---
title: "Fraud Detection & Risk Modeling"
description: "Detecting fraudulent transactions using statistical and machine learning techniques."
author: "Ananya Mahadeva"
date: today
categories: [Python,Statistics, Machine Learning, Fraud Analytics]
---

## 📌 Project Overview

This project simulates customer transaction data to detect fraudulent behavior using statistical techniques and machine learning models.

---

## 📊 Generate Dataset

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

np.random.seed(42)

n = 500

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "transactionamount": np.random.randint(100, 10000, n),
    "loanamount": np.random.randint(1000, 50000, n),
    "income": np.random.randint(20000, 100000, n),
    "creditscore": np.random.randint(300, 850, n),
    "isfraud": np.random.choice([0, 1], n)
})

df.head()
```

---

## 📈 Descriptive Statistics

```{python}
df.describe()
```

---

## ⚠️ Outlier Detection

```{python}
Q1 = df["transactionamount"].quantile(0.25)
Q3 = df["transactionamount"].quantile(0.75)
IQR = Q3 - Q1

outliers = df[(df["transactionamount"] < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR)) |
              (df["transactionamount"] > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))]

len(outliers)
```

---

## 📊 Fraud Distribution

```{python}
df["isfraud"].value_counts()
```

---

## 📉 Visualization

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.figure()
df["transactionamount"].hist()
plt.title("Transaction Amount Distribution")
plt.xlabel("Amount")
plt.ylabel("Frequency")
plt.show()
```

---

## 🤖 Machine Learning Model

```{python}
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

X = df.drop(columns=["isfraud"])
y = df["isfraud"]

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.25)

model = RandomForestClassifier()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)

pred = model.predict(X_test)
pred
```

---

## 📊 Model Accuracy

```{python}
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

accuracy_score(y_test, pred)
```

---

## 🔍 Key Insights

- Fraud detection is not dependent on a single variable  
- Transaction amount alone is insufficient  
- Multiple financial indicators improve prediction  

---

## 💼 Business Impact

- Helps detect fraud early  
- Reduces financial losses  
- Improves monitoring systems  

---

## 🚀 Conclusion

Machine learning models significantly improve fraud detection compared to traditional rule-based systems.
